mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS series over the prior quarter lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are sustained rather than a one-off adjustment.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive after sustained 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if triggered observations show negative median returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly acceleration in RSAFS above its recent trend indicates stronger nominal consumer demand, which lifts revenue expectations and supports S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when retail sales growth is broad rather than concentrated in one month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month average growth rate
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS acceleration does not correspond to higher median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relationship becomes negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE signals weakening labor demand and higher recession risk, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should appear when unemployment rises quickly rather than drifting sideways.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment spikes are followed by positive median 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns falls below 40% over triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with steady PAYEMS indicates real output growth without labor-market deterioration, which improves earnings visibility and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest when industrial production expands faster than payroll growth.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and stable PAYEMS does not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the combined signal has negative average returns in any 4-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated while FEDFUNDS remains low, real policy rates are deeply negative and equities benefit from easy financial conditions over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism fails once inflation decelerates sharply or policy turns restrictive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS is below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-high/policy-low regime does not outperform baseline SPX returns, or if positive 20-day returns occur less than 55% of the time in triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a previous peak signals housing demand rollover and weaker future spending on durable goods, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect is strongest when housing starts fall below their recent trend for several months.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts drawdowns do not precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the median forward return is not below zero after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, falling PAYEMS paired with rising UNRATE identifies broad employment loss and rising recession odds, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is not active when payrolls are flat and unemployment is unchanged.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll declines and unemployment increases do not lead to negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive for more than one rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising but GDP growth in GDPC1 remains weak, the economy is producing more near-term goods output without broad demand confirmation, which signals margin pressure and weaker equity performance over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when GDP and industrial production improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the divergence between INDPRO and GDPC1 does not predict lower 60-day SPX returns, or if joint-strength episodes produce the same negative outcome as divergence episodes.
